block-birdsnare-copper-desc: A snare with copper-wire noose. ~45% base catch chance. Crimson swifts (jungle), polar kebbits (cold), and other low-level prey.
block-birdsnare-tinbronze-desc: A snare with tin-bronze-wire noose. ~55% base catch chance. Pulls in golden warblers from desert biomes at Hunter L5+.
block-birdsnare-iron-desc: A snare with iron-wire noose. ~65% base catch chance. Copper longtails unlock at Hunter L9+.
block-birdsnare-steel-desc: A snare with steel-wire noose. ~72% base catch chance. Cerulean twitches unlock at Hunter L11+.
block-birdsnare-mithril-desc: A snare with mithril-wire noose. ~80% base catch chance. Tropical wagtails unlock at Hunter L19+.
block-birdsnare-adamantite-desc: A snare with adamantite-wire noose. ~87% base catch chance. High reliability.
block-birdsnare-runite-desc: A snare with runite-wire noose. ~94% base catch chance. Top-tier.
block-boxtrap-iron-desc: A baited wooden box with iron hinges. Small mammals in a 5×5 area approach. No snap-on-fail — keeps trying. ~50% catch chance.
block-boxtrap-mithril-desc: Mithril-hinged box trap. ~74% catch chance. Chinchompas in temperate biomes at Hunter L53+.
block-castingcradle-desc: Proto-form of the blast furnace. Tilts to pour molten metal into a mold below. v0.0.1.
item-devheatpellet-desc: Dev/testing fuel. Burns at 3000°C for 2 hours. Melts anything up to dragon tier. Not survival-craftable.
block-crucible-steel-fired-desc: A large steel-shelled crucible. 4x capacity of clay crucible. Melts steel and below; cannot reach mithril temperature.
block-crucible-adamantite-fired-desc: A larger adamantite-shelled crucible. 8x capacity. Melts adamantite and below; first bulk option for mithril. Cannot reach runite temperature.
block-crucible-dragon-fired-desc: The largest crucible. Dragon-shelled. 16x capacity. Melts all metals; first bulk option for runite.
block-handbooktext-castingcradle-*:
The casting cradle is the proto-form of the full blast furnace and the simplest way to pour molten metal at scale. Place it above an empty mold; when filled with molten metal it tilts and pours.

Useful as a stepping stone between hand-casting and the multiblock blast furnace. Survives steel-tier and below.
block-handbooktext-crucible-steel-fired:
Tier 1 of the high-temperature crucible ladder.

The steel-shelled crucible holds 4× the capacity of a clay crucible. It melts every metal up to and including steel.

Cannot reach mithril temperature. To work mithril and above, advance to the adamantite crucible.

The full ladder is: clay → steel → adamantite → dragon.
block-handbooktext-crucible-adamantite-f:
Tier 2 of the high-temperature crucible ladder.

The adamantite-shelled crucible holds 8× the capacity of a clay crucible. It is the first bulk option for mithril — every prior tier is hand-casting only or insufficient temperature.

Melts adamantite and below. Cannot reach runite temperature. To work runite and dragon, advance to the dragon crucible.

Crafting the adamantite shell requires mithril-tier or better tools and a working steel crucible to smelt the adamantite ingots in the first place — the ladder is self-bootstrapping but strictly sequential.
block-handbooktext-crucible-dragon-fired:
Tier 3 — top of the crucible ladder.

The dragon-shelled crucible holds 16× the capacity of a clay crucible and is the only crucible that melts every metal in the game, including runite and dragon.

It is the first bulk option for runite and the only option for dragon. Required to craft any dragon-tier item.

The shell itself requires dragon ingots, which means the crucible cannot be built until at least one dragon-tier hand-casting cycle has been completed using mithril/adamantite-tier infrastructure and a dev heat source — or, in survival, the full multiblock arc furnace.
item-handbooktext-ingot-mithril:
Tier 1 of the post-steel ladder. The first metal above vanilla steel, and the entry point into RuneScape Metals progression.

Source: mithril ore, found in veins below the surface in all stone biomes. Requires at least a steel pickaxe to mine.

Melting: requires the adamantite crucible or better — the steel crucible cannot reach mithril temperature.

Enables: full mithril tool and weapon coverage (pickaxe, axe, sword, halberd, etc.) and full mithril armor (chain, brigandine, lamellar, plate, scale, in all body slots). Mithril is also the minimum tier required to craft an adamantite crucible shell.
item-handbooktext-ingot-adamantite:
Tier 2 of the post-steel ladder. The mid-game metal — significantly tougher than mithril, the standard endgame for most non-completionist play.

Source: adamantite ore, found deeper than mithril and in more limited deposits. Requires at least a mithril pickaxe to mine.

Melting: the adamantite crucible is the first tier capable of melting it.

Enables: full adamantite tools, weapons, and armor across all suites. Adamantite is also the minimum tier required to craft a dragon crucible shell.
item-handbooktext-ingot-runite:
Tier 3 of the post-steel ladder. Rare and high-value; competitive endgame for serious play.

Source: runite ore, found only in deep deposits and rarer than adamantite. Requires at least an adamantite pickaxe to mine.

Melting: requires the dragon crucible — the adamantite crucible cannot reach runite temperature.

Enables: full runite tools, weapons, and armor. The runic pickaxe (if the Rune-Story integration mod is loaded) is the highest non-dragon mining tier.
item-handbooktext-ingot-dragon:
Tier 4 — the top of the ladder. The rarest and most powerful metal in the mod. Required for the dragon-tier special attacks.

Source: dragon ore, found only in the deepest deposits in extremely limited quantities. Requires at least a runite pickaxe to mine.

Melting: requires the dragon crucible — the only crucible that can reach dragon temperature. There is no higher option.

Enables: full dragon tools, weapons, and armor — the absolute ceiling. Dragon weapons unlock the dragon-tier special attacks (see individual weapon pages). Dragon is also the shell material for the dragon crucible itself; bootstrap requires either a dev heat pellet, the full multiblock arc furnace, or hand-casting at extreme temperatures.
block-handbooktext-ore-*-mithril-*:
Mithril ore — first metal above steel.

Required pickaxe: steel or better. Lower-tier pickaxes cannot break it.

Where to look: medium and deep underground in most stone biomes. Veins follow the usual VS grade ladder — poor → medium → rich → bountiful — with bountiful veins yielding roughly four times the metal-units of poor veins per block mined. Grade does not affect what you get, only how much.

Smelting: requires the adamantite crucible or better. Cannot be smelted in a steel crucible (temperature ceiling).
block-handbooktext-ore-*-adamantite-*:
Adamantite ore — second metal above steel; mid-game endgame for most play.

Required pickaxe: mithril or better.

Where to look: deeper than mithril veins; less common per biome. Grade ladder is the same poor → bountiful progression. Expect to mine substantially more rock per ingot than for mithril.

Smelting: requires the adamantite crucible (its native tier) or better.
block-handbooktext-ore-*-runite-*:
Runite ore — rare high-tier metal; serious endgame.

Required pickaxe: adamantite or better.

Where to look: only at significant depth and in noticeably rarer deposits than adamantite. Grade ladder applies but bountiful runite veins are themselves uncommon.

Smelting: requires the dragon crucible. The adamantite crucible cannot reach runite temperature.
block-handbooktext-ore-*-dragon-*:
Dragon ore — rarest metal in the mod; the absolute mining ceiling.

Required pickaxe: runite or better (a runic pickaxe, if Rune-Story integration is loaded, also qualifies).

Where to look: deepest tier of deposit; extremely limited per world. Bountiful dragon veins are a notable find.

Smelting: dragon crucible only. There is no higher option.
item-handbooktext-metallamellae-mithril:
Mithril lamella — a single scale of mithril armor plating.

Source: cast from molten mithril poured into a clay lamellae mold (12 scales per full mold). The mold accepts molten metal from either a hand-held crucible or a magma scoop; use the launder for larger pour volumes.

Use: combined in the crafting grid with a leather strap to assemble mithril lamellar armor (helmet, cuirass, or leggings) — each armor piece requires several lamellae in the standard vanilla lamellar pattern.

Also: reversible — a mithril lamella melts back to one mithril nugget-equivalent in an adamantite crucible or better. Useful for salvaging failed cycles.
item-handbooktext-metallamellae-adamanti:
Adamantite lamella — a scale of adamantite armor plating.

Source: cast from molten adamantite in a clay lamellae mold, poured from the adamantite crucible or better. Steel crucible cannot reach adamantite temperature.

Use: the adamantite lamellar armor tier, one of the stronger mid-game armors — better relative protection than mithril, similar durability floor to mithril plate.

Also: reversible via re-smelting in an adamantite crucible or better.
item-handbooktext-metallamellae-runite:
Runite lamella — a scale of runite armor plating.

Source: cast from molten runite in a clay lamellae mold. Requires the dragon crucible — no other crucible can reach runite temperature. Bulk pouring is best done through the magma scoop-and-launder chain.

Use: runite lamellar armor is the second-highest lamellar tier, with a protection floor comparable to full runite plate at slightly lower absorption.

Also: reversible in the dragon crucible.
item-handbooktext-metallamellae-dragon:
Dragon lamella — the top of the lamellar ladder.

Source: cast from molten dragon metal in a clay lamellae mold, poured from the dragon crucible (the only crucible capable of reaching dragon temperature).

Use: dragon lamellar armor is the highest armor tier in the mod, matched only by dragon plate in absolute protection. Lamellar tends to trade a small amount of raw absorption for higher relative protection scaling — a good pick if you expect to face high-damage single hits.

Also: reversible in the dragon crucible. Salvaging failed dragon-tier cycles is the primary reason to keep this reversible.
item-handbooktext-armor-*-lamellar-mithr:
Mithril lamellar armor. First lamellar tier above steel. Protection tier 6; ~54% flat damage reduction; 2700 durability per piece.

Made from: mithril lamellae + leather strap (vanilla lamellar recipe pattern). Cast lamellae in a clay lamellae mold from molten mithril; the adamantite crucible or better is required to melt mithril.

Compare: mithril plate has slightly higher raw absorption; mithril lamellar has slightly better relative-protection scaling and a lighter overall weight profile.
item-handbooktext-armor-*-lamellar-adama:
Adamantite lamellar armor. Second lamellar tier. Protection tier 7; ~62% flat damage reduction; 3050 durability per piece.

Made from: adamantite lamellae + leather strap.

Compare: adamantite plate is heavier and marginally more protective; adamantite lamellar breathes better in movement-heavy fights.
item-handbooktext-armor-*-lamellar-runit:
Runite lamellar armor. Third lamellar tier. Protection tier 8; ~73% flat damage reduction; 3500 durability per piece.

Made from: runite lamellae + leather strap. Runite lamellae require the dragon crucible to cast — no lower tier can reach runite temperature.

Compare: runite lamellar is the highest lamellar tier before dragon; it's a common endgame pick for players who don't want to commit to the dragon-crucible bootstrap.
item-handbooktext-armor-*-lamellar-drago:
Dragon lamellar armor — the ceiling of the lamellar ladder. Protection tier 9; ~87% flat damage reduction; 4050 durability per piece.

Made from: dragon lamellae + leather strap. Dragon lamellae are cast in a clay lamellae mold from molten dragon metal in a dragon crucible.

Compare: matched only by dragon plate. Lamellar trades a small amount of absolute reduction for the highest relative-protection floor in the mod — the best pick for surviving spike damage.
item-handbooktext-arrowshaft:
The foundation of fletching.

Cut from any wood log using a knife — left-click the log with a knife in hand. One log yields multiple shafts. All wood grades produce the same shaft; wood grade matters for bows, not arrows.

Next step: combine with feathers in a grid to produce a headless arrow, then add an arrowhead to finish.
item-handbooktext-headlessarrow:
A fletched but un-tipped arrow.

Made from an arrow shaft and feathers. Cannot be fired in this state — combine with any arrowhead (mithril, adamantite, runite, or dragon, plus vanilla tiers) in a grid to produce the finished tipped arrow.

One headless arrow + one arrowhead = one tipped arrow.
item-handbooktext-bowstring:
Bowstring for finishing unstrung bows.

Combine with an unstrung shortbow or longbow of any wood grade to produce the strung, fireable bow. The bowstring's source material is the same across all bow tiers — the bow's wood determines its stats, not the string.
item-handbooktext-arrow-*:
Tipped arrows for high-tier metals.

Each arrow is one headless arrow + one tier-matching arrowhead, combined in a crafting grid. Damage and armor penetration scale with the metal tier; the ladder above vanilla is mithril → adamantite → runite → dragon.

Tipped arrows fire from any bow whose draw weight matches or exceeds the arrow's tier. Lower-tier bows can fire higher-tier arrows but lose some of the arrow's damage potential.
item-handbooktext-shortbowu-*:
An unstrung shortbow.

Cut from a log of the named wood grade. Add a bowstring in the crafting grid to finish it. Shortbows draw faster and have higher fire rate than longbows of the same wood, at the cost of range and per-shot damage.

Wood ladder: common → oak → willow → maple → yew → magic. Each step up the wood ladder is a meaningful stat increase. Magic is the top tier and the rarest wood to source.
item-handbooktext-shortbow-*:
A strung shortbow, ready to fire.

Hold and rightmouse to draw; release to fire. Shortbows trade range for rate-of-fire compared to longbows of the same wood. Best paired with mid-tier metal arrows for sustained DPS at close to medium range.

Wood grade determines draw weight and damage — see the unstrung shortbow page for the wood tier ladder.
item-handbooktext-longbowu-*:
An unstrung longbow.

Cut from a log of the named wood grade. Add a bowstring in the crafting grid to finish it. Longbows draw slower and have lower fire rate than shortbows of the same wood, but reach farther and hit harder per shot.

Wood ladder: common → oak → willow → maple → yew → magic. Magic longbows are the highest-tier conventional ranged weapon in the mod.
item-handbooktext-longbow-*:
A strung longbow, ready to fire.

Hold and rightmouse to draw fully — longbows require a longer hold than shortbows. Release to fire. Pairs well with high-tier metal arrows for burst damage at long range.

Wood grade determines draw weight and damage — see the unstrung longbow page for the wood tier ladder.
itemdesc-arrowshaft: A wooden arrow shaft, cut from a log. Feathers next.
itemdesc-headlessarrow: Fletched and balanced — it wants an arrowhead.
itemdesc-bowstring: Spun flax, waxed and strong. It needs a stave.
itemdesc-shortbow-oak: A shortbow cut from oak. Sturdy and dependable.
itemdesc-shortbow-willow: An effective shortbow of supple waterside wood.
itemdesc-shortbow-yew: A fast bow cut from tough yew-grade timber.
itemdesc-shortbow-magic: A magical shortbow. The grain glints blue-green in the dark.
itemdesc-longbow-oak: A longbow of solid oak. Slow draw, true flight.
itemdesc-longbow-willow: A springy longbow with a forgiving draw.
itemdesc-longbow-maple: A well-balanced longbow of dense maple.
itemdesc-longbow-yew: The bowyer's pride. Wars were won with worse.
itemdesc-longbow-magic: A magical longbow. It hums faintly when drawn.
itemdesc-shortbowu-common: An unstrung shortbow stave. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-shortbowu-oak: An unstrung oak shortbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-shortbowu-willow: An unstrung willow shortbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-shortbowu-maple: An unstrung maple shortbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-shortbowu-yew: An unstrung yew shortbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-shortbowu-magic: An unstrung magic shortbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-longbowu-common: An unstrung longbow stave. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-longbowu-oak: An unstrung oak longbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-longbowu-willow: An unstrung willow longbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-longbowu-maple: An unstrung maple longbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-longbowu-yew: An unstrung yew longbow. Needs a bowstring.
itemdesc-longbowu-magic: An unstrung magic longbow. Needs a bowstring.
block-magmascoop-desc: Moves molten metal between large crucibles. Refuses any other liquid or container — a closed crucible-to-crucible courier.
item-smithcodex-desc: A unified codex covering every subsystem of the mod: metals ladder, crucible workflow, smithing, fletching, hunter, and integrations. Right-click to open.
block-superheater-desc: A magma-hearted booster housing. Place directly against a crucible machine's base to raise its maximum temperature by 300°C. Only one counts — they do not stack.
block-handbooktext-superheater:
The crucible ladder's missing rung.

Placed adjacent to any large crucible's base block, the superheater raises that crucible's ceiling by +300°C:

• Steel crucible (1600) → 1900: unlocks bulk mithril, still short of adamantite.
• Adamantite crucible (2100) → 2400: unlocks bulk runite, still short of dragon.
• Dragon crucible: headroom only — it already melts everything.

Multiple superheaters do not stack; the strongest single neighbor counts. Built from adamantite plate and rod around a charged magma scoop.
itemdesc-avasattractor: A back-worn contraption of magnetite, iron and stubborn cleverness. It tugs fired ammunition back to its owner and, left to its own devices, drags in stray iron oddments. Metal torso armor confounds it utterly.
block-launder-desc: A serpentine, sawtooth U-channel that carries molten metal from a large crucible into a waiting casting mold below. Dams and weirs slow the pour. Part of the bulk casting workflow.
block-af_controller-desc: Control block of the Tier-3 Arc Furnace multiblock. Ring it with the electrode array, lid, transformer and charge hatch, then interact here to smelt the highest-tier ores.
block-bf_controller-desc: Control block of the Tier-2 Blast Furnace multiblock. Build the chimney and tuyere around it, then interact here to smelt ore into metal.
itemdesc-metalframepack-mithril: A sturdy backpack rebuilt on a mithril frame. 10 slots. The frame carries what leather alone cannot.
itemdesc-metalframepack-adamantite: An adamantite-framed pack, upgraded from mithril. 12 slots.
itemdesc-metalframepack-runite: A runite-framed pack, upgraded from adamantite. 14 slots.
itemdesc-metalframepack-dragon: A dragon-framed pack, the last word in carrying. 16 slots. Where its frame goes, the load follows.
block-apiary-empty-desc: A home for your bees. Transplant a populated reed skep to settle a colony — this hive is permanent, and harvesting never destroys it.
block-apiary-populated-desc: Where bees live. The colony ripens on its own time, faster in a rich meadow. Needs flowers nearby.
block-apiary-harvestable-desc: The combs hang heavy. Right-click to harvest — the colony stays. 'Bee' good.
block-handbooktext-apiary-empty:
The permanent hive — the Catherby model.

Vanilla skeps die when you harvest them. The wooden skep does not: it is a permanent apiary. Break-proof husbandry — even demolishing it hands back the empty apiary block.

To settle a colony: let bees populate an ordinary reed skep as usual, then right-click the empty wooden skep holding the populated reed skep. The colony moves house and your woven skep is returned empty, ready to catch another swarm.

Bees need flowers: fewer than 3 bee-feed plants within 8 blocks and the colony will not ripen (the block info says so). A rich meadow ripens the combs up to 3× faster.

Harvest: when the skep drips, right-click — 2-4 honeycomb, and the colony stays. It re-ripens in roughly 1½ to 5½ days, forever.

'Bee' good.
block-handbooktext-apiary-populated:
The permanent hive — the Catherby model.

Vanilla skeps die when you harvest them. The wooden skep does not: it is a permanent apiary. Break-proof husbandry — even demolishing it hands back the empty apiary block.

To settle a colony: let bees populate an ordinary reed skep as usual, then right-click the empty wooden skep holding the populated reed skep. The colony moves house and your woven skep is returned empty, ready to catch another swarm.

Bees need flowers: fewer than 3 bee-feed plants within 8 blocks and the colony will not ripen (the block info says so). A rich meadow ripens the combs up to 3× faster.

Harvest: when the skep drips, right-click — 2-4 honeycomb, and the colony stays. It re-ripens in roughly 1½ to 5½ days, forever.

'Bee' good.
block-handbooktext-apiary-harvestable:
The permanent hive — the Catherby model.

Vanilla skeps die when you harvest them. The wooden skep does not: it is a permanent apiary. Break-proof husbandry — even demolishing it hands back the empty apiary block.

To settle a colony: let bees populate an ordinary reed skep as usual, then right-click the empty wooden skep holding the populated reed skep. The colony moves house and your woven skep is returned empty, ready to catch another swarm.

Bees need flowers: fewer than 3 bee-feed plants within 8 blocks and the colony will not ripen (the block info says so). A rich meadow ripens the combs up to 3× faster.

Harvest: when the skep drips, right-click — 2-4 honeycomb, and the colony stays. It re-ripens in roughly 1½ to 5½ days, forever.

'Bee' good.
item-handbooktext-metalframepack-mithril:
The carrying ladder.

Leather alone tops out at the sturdy backpack (8 slots). A metal frame carries what leather cannot:

• Mithril-framed — 10 slots
• Adamantite-framed — 12 slots
• Runite-framed — 14 slots
• Dragon-framed — 16 slots

Each pack is an upgrade: the previous bag sits at the centre of the grid, framed by that metal's plate above, rod below, and ordinary leather at the sides. The ladder starts from a plain leather backpack — the frame is the gate, not the hide.

Worn in a normal bag slot.
item-handbooktext-metalframepack-adamant:
The carrying ladder.

Leather alone tops out at the sturdy backpack (8 slots). A metal frame carries what leather cannot:

• Mithril-framed — 10 slots
• Adamantite-framed — 12 slots
• Runite-framed — 14 slots
• Dragon-framed — 16 slots

Each pack is an upgrade: the previous bag sits at the centre of the grid, framed by that metal's plate above, rod below, and ordinary leather at the sides. The ladder starts from a plain leather backpack — the frame is the gate, not the hide.

Worn in a normal bag slot.
item-handbooktext-metalframepack-runite:
The carrying ladder.

Leather alone tops out at the sturdy backpack (8 slots). A metal frame carries what leather cannot:

• Mithril-framed — 10 slots
• Adamantite-framed — 12 slots
• Runite-framed — 14 slots
• Dragon-framed — 16 slots

Each pack is an upgrade: the previous bag sits at the centre of the grid, framed by that metal's plate above, rod below, and ordinary leather at the sides. The ladder starts from a plain leather backpack — the frame is the gate, not the hide.

Worn in a normal bag slot.
item-handbooktext-metalframepack-dragon:
The carrying ladder.

Leather alone tops out at the sturdy backpack (8 slots). A metal frame carries what leather cannot:

• Mithril-framed — 10 slots
• Adamantite-framed — 12 slots
• Runite-framed — 14 slots
• Dragon-framed — 16 slots

Each pack is an upgrade: the previous bag sits at the centre of the grid, framed by that metal's plate above, rod below, and ordinary leather at the sides. The ladder starts from a plain leather backpack — the frame is the gate, not the hide.

Worn in a normal bag slot.
block-handbooktext-launder:
The heavy pour channel.

Place the launder directly north of a crucible machine's base, at the same level. When the drum tips, molten metal drains down the sawtooth meander — 5 units a second — into the launder's buffer (16 units), and onward to casting.

The channel rejects mixed metals: one melt, one metal, one pour.

Placed like any block; the meander now stands proud on top of the ground where you can see it run.
block-apiarysection-base-desc: Four legs and a nailed frame. The bottom of a proper hive.
block-apiarysection-body-desc: A wax-primed brood box. The heart of a proper hive.
block-apiarysection-roof-desc: A flared lid to keep the rain off the combs.
item-apiaryscoop-desc: A long wooden scoop for lifting combs and wax from a living hive without enraging it. Weekly rounds.
